summary: The implementation covers most core requirements including 3D racing with Three.js/React Three Fiber, 10 playable characters, racing mechanics (acceleration, braking, drifting), race state management, backend leaderboards, and vibrant UI styling. However, several acceptance criteria are missing or not evidenced in the diff, particularly power-up collection/usage mechanics, and some visual design elements.

missing_requirements.requirement: Power-up items can be collected and used to gain advantages
missing_requirements.reason: PowerUpItem component exists but lacks collection logic integration with PlayerKart. The onCollect callback is defined but there's no evidence of collision detection between kart and power-ups, nor item usage implementation.

missing_requirements.requirement: Items can be used to hinder opponents
missing_requirements.reason: No opponent karts exist in the implementation (as per non-goals), but more critically, no projectile or hindrance mechanics are implemented even for visual/gameplay effects.

Backend stores username but there's no evidence in the diff of player authentication or identity integration with the race completion flow. The useInternetIdentity hook exists but no connection to saveRaceDetails is shown.
Limited animation evidence beyond basic rotation and bounce effects. No transition animations, particle effects, or energetic UI animations are shown in the truncated code.

missing_requirements.requirement: Visual theme is consistent across all screens
missing_requirements.reason: Only partial screen implementations are visible. Leaderboard and RaceResults pages are referenced but not shown in the diff, making consistency verification impossible.

hallucinated_features.description: Checkpoint-based lap tracking system with 4 checkpoints per lap
hallucinated_features.reason: REQ-3 specifies lap counter functionality but doesn't require a checkpoint system. The implementation adds complexity with a 4-checkpoint validation mechanism not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: Physics engine with velocity tracking and lerp-based movement
hallucinated_features.reason: While REQ-1 requires physics-based movement, the implementation includes a full custom physics hook (usePhysics) that isn't shown in the diff but is imported, suggesting additional complexity beyond requirements.
REQ-3 requires power-ups to appear on track but doesn't specify respawn mechanics. This adds game loop complexity not requested.
